verdict_reasoning: ABCB9 (TAPL) is a half-type ABC transporter localized exclusively to lysosomal membranes. Multiple studies using GFP fusion, immunofluorescence, and subcellular fractionation confirm colocalization with lysosomal markers (LAMP1/LAMP2, cathepsin D). The protein's N-terminal transmembrane domain (TMD0) is essential for lysosomal targeting and prevents surface residence; truncation studies show that only the N-terminal region lacking this TMD permits plasma membrane accumulation. No surface pool, cell-state-induced ecto-display, tissue-restricted surface display, lysosomal exocytosis, dual localization, or stable surface attachment is documented. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic targets ABCB9. The protein is endomembrane-resident only.
